perturbation.original_text: 12. Applicable Law an d Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the Republic of Italy without regard to Italy’s conflicts of law rules . Any disputes or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be submi tted to the sole competence and jurisdiction of the ordinary Courts of Trieste .
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Secrecy The existence of this Agreement cannot be disclosed to any third party . It is thus forbidden to make any oral and/or written disclosure (advertising, publication, or any other kind of external 5/5 communication) of this Agreement being in force nor of the potential c o-operation that could be subsequently negotiated , without prio r and written authorisation of all Parties. 12. Applicable Law an d Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the Republic of Italy without regard to Italy’s conflicts of law rules . Any disputes or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be submi tted to the sole competence and jurisdiction of the ordinary Courts of Trieste .
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Applicable Law and Jurisdiction' clause to the 'Secrecy' section makes it less discoverable and harder to enforce, as it's no longer in a standard location for legal terms.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Italian Code of Civil Procedure, Article 12
perturbation.law_citation: Italian Code of Civil Procedure, Article 12
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 12 of the Italian Code of Civil Procedure emphasizes the importance of interpreting laws according to their intended meaning. By burying the governing law and jurisdiction clause in an unrelated section about secrecy, the contract obscures the parties' intentions regarding dispute resolution, potentially contradicting the spirit of Article 12 and hindering a court's ability to properly interpret the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the 'Applicable Law and Jurisdiction' clause to the 'Secrecy' section makes it less discoverable and harder to enforce. The Law Explanation supports this, stating that the contract obscures the parties' intentions regarding dispute resolution, potentially hindering a court's ability to properly interpret the agreement. The definition of the Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction perturbation also supports this, noting that it arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. Therefore, the relocation of the clause contradicts legal standards.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of information and property The Receiving Party acknowledge s and ag rees tha t the Confidential Information is and remains the property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving party must, at the end of this Agreement or within seven days following written instruction by the Disclosing Party to do so, return or destroy all Confidential Information in the Receiving Party 's possession, custody or control. The Receiving Party may retain one (1) copy of any such Confidential Information to be stored securely with its legal representative only if required for regulatory or insura nce purposes , but in this event the confidentiality and non -use provisions in t his Agreement remain in place with respect to such retained Confidential I nformation. The return or destruction of any Confidential Information will not diminish or otherwise affect any other obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Secrecy The existence of this Agreement cannot be disclosed to any third party . It is thus forbidden to make any oral and/or written disclosure (advertising, publication, or any other kind of external 5/5 communication) of this Agreement being in force nor of the potential c o-operation that could be subsequently negotiated , without prio r and written authorisation of all Parties. 5. Return of information and property The Receiving Party acknowledge s and ag rees tha t the Confidential Information is and remains the property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving party must, at the end of this Agreement or within seven days following written instruction by the Disclosing Party to do so, return or destroy all Confidential Information in the Receiving Party 's possession, custody or control. The Receiving Party may retain one (1) copy of any such Confidential Information to be stored securely with its legal representative only if required for regulatory or insura nce purposes , but in this event the confidentiality and non -use provisions in t his Agreement remain in place with respect to such retained Confidential I nformation. The return or destruction of any Confidential Information will not diminish or otherwise affect any other obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Return of information and property' clause into the 'Secrecy' section obscures the obligations related to the return or destruction of confidential information upon termination or request, making it less accessible and easily overlooked.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1362 of the Italian Civil Code deals with the interpretation of contracts, emphasizing the common intention of the parties. By moving the clause about returning confidential information into the secrecy section, one party is obscuring the conditions related to the return of the confidential material, thus, obfuscating the conditions when the receiving party must perform such action, potentially undermining the ascertainment of the true intention of the parties at the moment of the contract's conclusion regarding the return and use of confidential material.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Italian Civil Code - Wikipedia Jump to content From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia The Italian Civil Code ( Italian : Codice civile ) is the civil code of Italy, a collection of norms regulating private law . [ 1 ] It was enacted under Fascist rule , by Royal decree no. 262 of 16 March 1942. It predates the current Italian Constitution , and it was amended in the postwar period. [ 1 ] The 1942 civil code replaced an earlier civil code which was in force since 1865, the latter being essentially an Italian translation of the Napoleonic Code . [ 1 ] Some parts of the 1942 code are based on the German Civil Code of 1900. Contents [ edit ] Preliminary provisions: 16 provisions applying to all books of the civil code. Book 1 - family law: marriage, adoption. Book 2 - inheritance law: wills. Book 3 - property: movable items, real estate, property rights and limits. Book 4 - bonds, purchase and sale contracts, mortgages. Book 5 - business law and labor law. Book 6 - civil liability, transcription, credit law, rules of evidence. References [ edit ] ^ a b c Perlingieri, Pietro (2017). Manuale di diritto civile (8 ed.). Napoli: Edizioni scientifiche italiane. perturbation.justification: The 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction' perturbation type indicates that a legal contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section. The modified text moves the 'Return of information and property' clause into the 'Secrecy' section. The Law Explanation states that Article 1362 of the Italian Civil Code deals with interpreting contracts based on the parties' common intention. By obscuring the conditions for returning confidential material, the changed text obfuscates when the receiving party must perform that action, potentially undermining the true intention of the parties. Scraped Law Snippet 2 mentions the Italian Civil Code, which supports the legal basis for contract interpretation. The relocation of the clause creates a structural flaw, making it harder to locate and thus contradicting the intent of clear contract interpretation.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Duration This Agreement shall terminate 5 (five) years after the Effective Date (the “ Termination Date ”). Notwithstanding this, any Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement by giving 30 (thirty) days written notice (registered letter with proof of receipt) to the other Parties. Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any obligation imposed by this Agreement with 4/5 respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination , the obligations of non -use and non-disclosure of Confidential Information shall survive for 3 (three) years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information. After the term of this Agreement has ended, the Disclosing Party does not relinquish an y other rights that it may have under copyright, patent, or other intellectual property laws.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Secrecy The existence of this Agreement cannot be disclosed to any third party . It is thus forbidden to make any oral and/or written disclosure (advertising, publication, or any other kind of external 5/5 communication) of this Agreement being in force nor of the potential c o-operation that could be subsequently negotiated , without prio r and written authorisation of all Parties. 10. Duration This Agreement shall terminate 5 (five) years after the Effective Date (the “ Termination Date ”). Notwithstanding this, any Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement by giving 30 (thirty) days written notice (registered letter with proof of receipt) to the other Parties. Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any obligation imposed by this Agreement with 4/5 respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination , the obligations of non -use and non-disclosure of Confidential Information shall survive for 3 (three) years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information. After the term of this Agreement has ended, the Disclosing Party does not relinquish an y other rights that it may have under copyright, patent, or other intellectual property laws.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Duration' clause to the 'Secrecy' section would mean hiding very important terms regarding the contract's duration and how termination may occur. Important clauses like these require proper placing.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1350 of the Italian Civil Code requires certain contracts involving real estate to be in writing, but the underlying principle emphasizes the need for clarity and accessibility of crucial contractual terms. While Article 1350 is not directly about NDA agreements it showcases the importance of well defined and accessible terms in a written document. Moving the 'Duration' and 'Termination' clauses into a secrecy provision acts against the principle of clarity and accessible agreement terms, therefore contradicting a key principle of Article 1350 regarding the clarity that should be present in written agreements.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves relocating the 'Duration' clause into a 'Secrecy' section, which undermines the principle of clarity and accessibility of crucial contract terms. The Law Explanation points out that Article 1350 of the Italian Civil Code, while not directly about NDA agreements, emphasizes the need for clarity and accessible terms. This is echoed in Scraped Law Snippet 1, which shows Article 1350 within the broader context of contract law under the Italian Civil Code. By hiding the 'Duration' clause within a section on secrecy, the modified text creates a structural flaw that obstructs the accessibility of important contractual information, contradicting the principle of clarity, leading to a legal contradiction.
